title: Getting Data Out 📤
description: Make your functions hand back answers using Return values.

slides.title: To Void or Not to Void 🌌
slides.content:
# The Output Tube

Until now, every function has started with `void`. `void` means 'Nothing'!
`void calculate()` means the function will do some work entirely on its own, and not hand any physical data back to who called it.

But what if we want the function to calculate some math, and hand the REAL ANSWER back out to a variable?

slides.title: Replacing 'void' 🔄

quizData.question: What is the opposite of a 'void' function?

options.text: A function that has a specific return type, like 'int' or 'String'.

quizData.explanation: Functions either return nothing (void) or they return a specific data type like int, double, bool, or String.

slides.title: Catching the Return ⚾

quizData.explanation: The 'return' keyword instantly spits the value out of the function and terminates it.

slides.title: See it in Action
slides.content: Notice how we catch the `bool` result and use it in an `if` block!
